more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Who will drive the bus: Mamvura, General Chiwenga, or someone else? Will President Mnangagwa retire peacefully? (Part 2)
more.description: In this second and final part of the article, I continue to examine the potential outcomes of ZANU-PF’s succession politics, focusing on whether Kudakwashe Tagwirei (whom I metaphorically refer to as “Mamvura”) will succeed in his presumed bid for the presidency, whether General Constantino Chiwenga will recover his political standing and take over, whether someone else will ascend to the throne, and whether President Mnangagwa will ultimately retire in peace. The post Who will drive the bus: Mamvura, General Chiwenga, or someone else? Will President Mnangagwa retire peacefully? (Part 2) appeared first on Nehanda Radio .

more.title: Biti’s arrest a cowardly act says Ugandan opposition leader Bobi Wine
more.description: EXILED Ugandan opposition leader Robert Kyagulanyi Ssentamu popularly known as Bobi Wine has condemned the arrest of former legislator Tendai Biti, slamming the Zimbabwean government of shrinking the democratic space. Biti, a convener of the Constitution Defenders Forum (CDF) together with a member Morgan Ncube, was arrested in Mutare over the weekend as they campaigned [...] The post Biti’s arrest a cowardly act says Ugandan opposition leader Bobi Wine appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .
